description_long: This is a grand stone bank building elevated on a robust stone platform, featuring a classical architectural design with a symmetrical facade. The front is dominated by a portico with four large fluted columns supporting a triangular pediment, and two side wings with matching gabled roofs. The roof is covered with reddish-brown terracotta tiles, and the walls are constructed from light-colored stone blocks, showing signs of weathering. The windows are small, rectangular, and shuttered, with decorative stone lintels and sills. The main entrance is centrally located, flanked by the columns, and features double wooden doors with ornate carvings. The structure exudes a sense of permanence and security, typical of neoclassical bank buildings.
description: A grand neoclassical stone bank with a columned portico, terracotta-tiled roof, and elevated stone base. The facade features ornate details, shuttered windows, and a prominent double-door entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the imposing columned portico, double wooden doors, and symmetrical wings with gabled roofs.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the building, tiled roof, and small shuttered windows along the stone wall.
description_view_2: Rear view with three small shuttered windows, plain stonework, and the full expanse of the tiled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the building's length, roof structure, and stone base.
attribute_entrance_design: Central double wooden doors beneath a columned portico, featuring ornate carvings and flanked by large stone columns with a triangular pediment above.
